Summary Tier 1 Tier 2 Tier 3 Safety Citations
Summary Tier 1 Tier 2 Tier 3 Safety Citations
Last reviewed: 2026-03-24

Supplement Safety, Drug Interactions & Contraindications

A systematic review of 113 supplements across 402+ research citations, ranked by evidence strength.

This guide summarizes published research. It does not constitute medical advice. Full disclaimer.

Share this guide
402+ Verified Citations FDA-Compliant Language No Industry Funding Our Methodology

Quick Evidence Summary

Supplement Tier Key Mechanism Primary Finding Dosage
Ashwagandha Strong Evidence HPA axis modulation Stress reduction 300-600mg daily (standardized extract; 600mg/day optimal for stress/anxiety per dose-response MA)
B-Complex Vitamins Strong Evidence Neurotransmitter synthesis support Energy metabolism RDA levels to therapeutic doses (varies by B vitamin)
Caffeine Strong Evidence Adenosine receptor antagonism Exercise performance 50-400mg daily
Citicoline Strong Evidence Phosphatidylcholine synthesis enhancement Neuroprotection 250-1000mg daily
Creatine Strong Evidence ATP regeneration Muscle strength 3-5g daily (maintenance) or 20g daily for 5 days (loading)
Folate Strong Evidence One-carbon metabolism via MTHFR/DHFR enzymatic pathway Neural tube development support 400–800 mcg/day standard; 800 mcg–5 mg/day for special populations (MTHFR carriers, therapeutic depression, cerebral folate deficiency)
Iron Strong Evidence Oxygen transport via hemoglobin Oxygen transport 8-65mg daily; RDA 8-18mg; therapeutic 25-65mg; athletes 100mg/day; bisglycinate preferred
Magnesium Strong Evidence GABA receptor modulation Sleep enhancement 200-400mg daily (elemental magnesium)
Melatonin Strong Evidence Melatonin receptor activation Sleep onset 0.5-3mg, 30-60 minutes before bedtime
Omega-3 Fatty Acids Strong Evidence Cell membrane fluidity Cardiovascular health 1000-2000mg daily (combined EPA/DHA)
Turmeric/Curcumin Strong Evidence NF-κB pathway inhibition Anti-inflammatory 500-1000mg daily (with piperine for absorption)
Vitamin B12 Strong Evidence Myelin sheath maintenance Red blood cell formation 2.4μg RDA to 1000μg therapeutic
Vitamin C Strong Evidence Powerful antioxidant activity (MDA reduction, TAC increase, GPx enhancement) Immune system support 500mg-2g daily; optimal antioxidant and immune benefit at 500-1000mg
Vitamin D3 Strong Evidence Vitamin D receptor activation Bone health 1000-4000 IU daily (based on blood levels; 2000-4000 IU optimal for metabolic benefit)
Zinc Strong Evidence Enzyme cofactor (300+ enzymes and 2000+ proteins) Immune system support 8-40mg daily; therapeutic range 15-30mg; bisglycinate preferred for absorption
5-HTP Moderate Evidence Serotonin synthesis precursor — bypasses rate-limiting tryptophan hydroxylase step Serotonin synthesis 50-300mg daily
Acetyl-L-Carnitine Moderate Evidence Mitochondrial energy production Depression support 500-2000mg daily
Akkermansia muciniphila Moderate Evidence Amuc_1100 outer membrane protein activation of TLR2 signaling to strengthen gut barrier tight junctions Insulin sensitivity improvement 1×10^9 – 1×10^10 CFU/day (pasteurized or live)
Alpha-GPC Moderate Evidence Acetylcholine synthesis enhancement Growth hormone support 300-600mg daily
Alpha-Lipoic Acid Moderate Evidence Universal antioxidant (water & fat soluble) Blood sugar control 100-600mg daily
Bacopa monnieri Moderate Evidence Acetylcholinesterase inhibition Anxiety reduction 300mg daily (standardized to 55% bacosides)
Berberine Moderate Evidence AMPK pathway activation Blood sugar regulation 500-1500mg daily (divided doses)
Bitter Melon Moderate Evidence Alpha-glucosidase inhibition Blood glucose reduction 500-2000mg daily (extract), 50-100ml fresh juice
Black Seed Oil Moderate Evidence Thymoquinone antioxidant activity Immune system support 500-2000mg daily (oil or extract)
Boswellia Moderate Evidence 5-lipoxygenase inhibition Joint health 300-800mg daily (standardized extract)
Bovine Colostrum Moderate Evidence Immunoglobulin (IgG, IgA) transfer providing passive immunity Upper respiratory infection prevention 0.5-20 g/day
Centella Asiatica Moderate Evidence Improved cerebral circulation Circulation improvement 300-900mg daily (standardized extract)
Choline Moderate Evidence Acetylcholine synthesis precursor Liver health 250mg-2g daily
Chondroitin Sulfate Moderate Evidence Cartilage matrix MMP/ADAMTS suppression Joint health support 800-1200mg daily (pharmaceutical-grade CS only)
Chromium Moderate Evidence Enhanced insulin receptor binding Improved insulin sensitivity 200-400mcg daily
Cinnamon Extract Moderate Evidence Enhanced insulin sensitivity Blood glucose reduction 120mg-6g daily (extract or powder)
Collagen Peptides Moderate Evidence Stimulation of fibroblast proliferation and collagen synthesis in dermis Skin hydration, elasticity, and wrinkle reduction 2.5-15 g/day (most common: 5-10 g/day)
CoQ10 Moderate Evidence Mitochondrial energy production Heart health 100-300mg daily
Cordyceps Moderate Evidence ATP and oxygen utilization enhancement Exercise performance 1-3g daily
Elderberry Moderate Evidence Antiviral hemagglutinin inhibition Immune system enhancement 300-600mg daily (standardized extract)
Fenugreek Moderate Evidence Alpha-amylase inhibition Blood glucose regulation 500-1000mg daily (seed extract)
Garlic Moderate Evidence Allicin and sulfur compounds Cardiovascular health 600mg-2g daily
Ginger Moderate Evidence COX and LOX inhibition Nausea reduction 500mg-2g daily
Ginkgo Biloba Moderate Evidence Cerebral blood flow enhancement Peripheral circulation 120-240mg daily (standardized extract EGb 761)
Glucosamine Moderate Evidence Cartilage matrix GAG and proteoglycan synthesis (in vitro evidence) Joint pain reduction (contested) 1500mg glucosamine sulfate daily (sulfate form preferred over HCl)
Glutathione Moderate Evidence Direct scavenging of reactive oxygen species (ROS) and reactive nitrogen species (RNS) Master antioxidant replenishment and oxidative stress reduction 250-1000 mg/day oral; 500-1000 mg/day liposomal
GlyNAC Moderate Evidence Glutathione synthesis restoration via rate-limiting precursors glycine and cysteine Glutathione repletion and antioxidant defense restoration 2.4-14 g/day total GlyNAC (1:1 glycine:NAC ratio)
Grape Seed Extract Moderate Evidence Potent proanthocyanidin antioxidants Cardiovascular health 100-400mg daily (standardized extract)
Green Tea Extract Moderate Evidence EGCG multi-target cellular signaling (NF-κB, MAPK, PI3K/Akt inhibition) Weight management 200-400mg EGCG daily (take with food)
Gymnema Sylvestre Moderate Evidence Gymnemic acid sugar blocking Blood sugar regulation 200-800mg daily (standardized extract)
Hawthorn Berry Moderate Evidence ACE inhibition Heart health support 160-1800mg daily (standardized extract)
Holy Basil Moderate Evidence HPA axis modulation Cortisol regulation 300-600mg daily (standardized extract)
Huperzine A Moderate Evidence Acetylcholinesterase inhibition Alzheimer's disease support 50-200mcg daily
Inositol Moderate Evidence Second messenger system component PCOS management 2-18g daily
Kanna Moderate Evidence Serotonin reuptake inhibition Mood enhancement 25-100mg daily (standardized extract)
Krill Oil Moderate Evidence Phospholipid-bound omega-3 delivery Cardiovascular health 500-2000mg daily
L-Theanine Moderate Evidence GABA receptor modulation Anxiety reduction 100-200mg daily (often with caffeine)
Lion's Mane Mushroom Moderate Evidence NGF (Nerve Growth Factor) stimulation Digestive health 500-1000mg daily (standardized extract)
Lutein Moderate Evidence Macular pigment accumulation Macular health support 6-20mg daily
Maca Root Moderate Evidence Adaptogenic regulation of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis Sexual desire enhancement in healthy men and women 1,500-3,500 mg/day (gelatinized or dried root powder)
Magnesium L-Threonate Moderate Evidence Elevation of brain magnesium concentration via BBB-permeable threonate transport Sleep quality improvement 1,000-2,000 mg/day magnesium L-threonate
MCT Oil Moderate Evidence Rapid ketone body production Weight management 5-30ml daily
Methylene Blue Moderate Evidence Mitochondrial electron carrier (alternative electron shuttle from NADH to cytochrome c) Neuroprotection 0.5-4 mg/kg/day oral (15-280 mg depending on indication)
Milk Thistle Moderate Evidence Antioxidant liver protection Liver protection 200-600mg daily (80% silymarin extract)
Moringa Moderate Evidence Dense nutritional profile Comprehensive nutrition 500-1500mg daily (leaf powder/extract)
Mucuna Pruriens Moderate Evidence L-DOPA dopamine precursor Testosterone support 500-1000mg daily (15% L-DOPA extract)
NAD+ Precursors Moderate Evidence NAD+ biosynthesis via Preiss-Handler pathway (gut microbiota conversion) Mitochondrial function 250-500mg NR or NMN daily
NMN (Nicotinamide Mononucleotide) Moderate Evidence NAD+ biosynthesis precursor (direct substrate for NMNAT enzymes) Increased blood NAD+ levels 250-900 mg/day; most evidence at 250 mg/day for 8-12 weeks
Palmitoylethanolamide Moderate Evidence PPAR-alpha receptor activation Chronic pain reduction across neuropathic, musculoskeletal, and nociceptive subtypes 300-1200 mg/day
Panax Ginseng Moderate Evidence HPA axis modulation Physical performance 200-400mg daily (standardized extract)
Passionflower Moderate Evidence GABA receptor enhancement Sleep quality 250-800mg daily (extract), 4-8g dried herb
Phosphatidylserine Moderate Evidence Cell membrane fluidity optimization Stress hormone reduction 100-300mg daily
Pine Bark Extract Moderate Evidence Potent antioxidant activity Cardiovascular health 50-200mg daily (standardized extract)
Piracetam Moderate Evidence Enhanced neuronal membrane fluidity Circulation improvement 2400-4800mg daily (where legal)
Quercetin Moderate Evidence Senescent cell clearance Senolytic activity 500-1000mg daily
Red Yeast Rice Moderate Evidence HMG-CoA reductase inhibition Reduced vascular cognitive impairment risk 1200-2400mg daily (standardized extract)
Reishi Mushroom Moderate Evidence Beta-glucan immune modulation Immune system support 1-3g daily (extract) or 6-12g dried
Resveratrol Moderate Evidence NF-κB pathway inhibition (anti-inflammatory) Anti-inflammatory (CRP/TNF-α reduction) 150-500mg daily (75mg twice daily for cognitive outcomes)
Rhodiola rosea Moderate Evidence HPA axis modulation Physical fatigue reduction 200-400mg daily (standardized extract)
Schisandra Berry Moderate Evidence Liver enzyme induction Liver detoxification 500-1500mg daily (standardized extract)
Selenium Moderate Evidence Glutathione peroxidase cofactor Thyroid function 55-200mcg daily
Spirulina Moderate Evidence High protein and micronutrient content Immune support 1-8g daily
Stinging Nettle Moderate Evidence Histamine receptor antagonism Allergy relief 300-600mg daily (leaf extract)
Sulbutiamine Moderate Evidence Enhanced thiamine bioavailability Physical energy increase 200-600mg daily
Tongkat Ali Moderate Evidence HPA axis modulation (cortisol reduction) Testosterone enhancement 200 mg/day (standardized aqueous root extract, 1-2% eurycomanone)
Urolithin A Moderate Evidence Mitophagy activation via PINK1/Parkin pathway Muscle strength improvement 500-1000 mg/day
Vitamin B6 Moderate Evidence Neurotransmitter synthesis (serotonin, dopamine, GABA) Protein metabolism 1.3-1.7mg RDA to 50-100mg therapeutic
Vitamin E Moderate Evidence Lipid peroxidation inhibition (chain-breaking antioxidant in cell membranes and LDL) Immune function enhancement (elderly) 15mg/day RDA; 200 IU/day for immune benefit; 2000 IU/day for AD under specialist supervision; CAUTION: ≥400 IU/day increases all-cause mortality
Whey Protein Moderate Evidence mTOR/p70S6K1 pathway activation via leucine Muscle protein synthesis 20-40g post-exercise; 40g/day for muscle maintenance in elderly
Zeaxanthin Moderate Evidence Macular pigment density increase Macular health support 2-10mg daily
Aniracetam Preliminary Evidence AMPA receptor positive modulation Mood enhancement 750-1500mg daily (where legal)
Apigenin Preliminary Evidence GABA-A receptor modulation (benzodiazepine binding site agonist) Anxiety reduction (via chamomile extract) 50-100mg daily (isolated); or 500-1500mg chamomile extract standardized to 1.2% apigenin
Astaxanthin Preliminary Evidence Potent antioxidant activity Skin health 4-12mg daily
Beta-Alanine Preliminary Evidence Carnosine synthesis in skeletal muscle Exercise endurance 4-6g daily (split doses)
Betaine Preliminary Evidence Osmolyte function for cellular hydration Power output enhancement 2.5g daily
Chlorella Preliminary Evidence Chlorophyll detoxification support Heavy metal detox 3-10g daily
Cistanche Preliminary Evidence Neuroprotection via echinacoside-mediated BDNF and GDNF upregulation Chronic fatigue symptom reduction 300-450 mg/day (standardized extract with echinacoside)
Citrulline Malate Preliminary Evidence Arginine-nitric oxide pathway enhancement Nitric oxide production 6-8g acute pre-workout
DIM (3,3'-Diindolylmethane) Preliminary Evidence Modulates estrogen metabolism by shifting 2-hydroxylation:16a-hydroxylation ratio via CYP1A1/CYP1A2 induction Favorable estrogen metabolism (increased 2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 ratio) 100-300mg daily (absorption-enhanced formulation); typical clinical dose 150mg BID
DMAE Preliminary Evidence Choline precursor activity Mood improvement 100-300mg daily
Echinacea Preliminary Evidence Immune system modulation Immune system support 300mg-1g daily
Fisetin Preliminary Evidence Selective elimination of senescent cells via PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway inhibition Senolytic activity (clearance of senescent cells) 100-500 mg/day (continuous); 20 mg/kg/day x 2 days (senolytic protocol)
Forskolin Preliminary Evidence Adenylyl cyclase activation Metabolic enhancement 250-500mg daily (10% forskolin extract)
GABA Preliminary Evidence Primary inhibitory neurotransmitter (GABA-A and GABA-B receptor agonism) Muscle relaxation 100-750mg daily (stress/anxiety); 500-750mg pre-bedtime (sleep)
HMB (β-Hydroxy β-Methylbutyrate) Preliminary Evidence mTOR pathway activation for protein synthesis Muscle protein synthesis 3g daily or 38mg/kg body weight
L-Tyrosine Preliminary Evidence Dopamine synthesis precursor (rate-limiting substrate) Catecholamine synthesis 100–150 mg/kg bodyweight (acute) or 2g twice daily (chronic)
MSM Preliminary Evidence NF-κB pathway inhibition reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine production Osteoarthritis joint pain reduction (contested) 2000-3375mg daily
Nattokinase Preliminary Evidence Direct fibrinolysis via serine protease enzymatic activity Blood pressure reduction 2,000-4,000 FU/day (up to 8,000 FU/day studied)
PEA (Phenylethylamine) Preliminary Evidence Dopamine and norepinephrine release Weight management 100-500mg daily
Pentadecanoic Acid (C15:0) Preliminary Evidence Dual PPARalpha/delta partial agonism Cardiometabolic risk reduction 100-200 mg daily (pure C15:0; FA15)
PQQ Preliminary Evidence Mitochondrial biogenesis via PGC-1α upregulation Mitochondrial biogenesis (PGC-1α upregulation) 20mg daily
Pterostilbene Preliminary Evidence Enhanced bioavailability vs resveratrol Antioxidant effects 50-250mg daily
Shilajit Preliminary Evidence Fulvic acid-mediated mitochondrial electron transport enhancement Testosterone and androgenic hormone support 250-500 mg/day of standardized purified extract
Spermidine Preliminary Evidence Autophagy induction via eIF5A hypusination and TFEB translation Cardiovascular protection 1-6 mg/day (wheat germ extract)
Taurine Preliminary Evidence Calcium regulation in cells Cardiovascular health 1-6g daily (endurance performance); 0.5-1.5g daily (cardiovascular/general health)
Tribulus Terrestris Preliminary Evidence Steroidal saponin (protodioscin) activity — potential LH and testosterone modulation Libido support (modest, especially women) 250-750mg daily (standardized to 40-45% saponins)
Vanadium Preliminary Evidence Protein tyrosine phosphatase inhibition Insulin mimetic effects 10-100mg daily (vanadyl sulfate)
Vinpocetine Preliminary Evidence Cerebral vasodilation Stroke recovery 5-20mg daily

Introduction

Dietary supplements are widely used but not without risk. Drug-supplement interactions, contraindications for specific populations, and adverse effects are real concerns that deserve the same evidence-based scrutiny applied to pharmaceutical agents.

This guide provides a comprehensive safety reference for all supplements in the SupplementDB database. Unlike our domain-specific guides that focus on efficacy, this resource prioritizes safety information: known drug interactions, contraindicated populations, dose-dependent adverse effects, and regulatory considerations.

Critical Notice: This guide is for informational purposes only and does not constitute medical advice. Always disclose all supplement use to your healthcare providers, including physicians, pharmacists, and anesthesiologists. Drug-supplement interactions can have serious — and in some cases life-threatening — consequences. When in doubt, consult a qualified healthcare professional before starting or combining any supplement.

Safety & Interactions supplements This comprehensive safety guide covers drug interactions, contraindications, and adverse effect profiles for all 93 supplements in the SupplementDB database. Supplements with the most significant interaction potential include 5-HTP (serotonin syndrome risk), berberine (CYP450 inhibition), ginkgo biloba (bleeding risk), and kanna (SSRI contraindication).

All Supplements: Safety & Interaction Profiles

  1. Ashwagandha Strong Evidence — Stress reduction (300-600mg daily (standardized extract; 600mg/day optimal for stress/anxiety per dose-response MA))
  2. Omega-3 Fatty Acids Strong Evidence — Cardiovascular health (1000-2000mg daily (combined EPA/DHA))
  3. 5-HTP Moderate Evidence — Serotonin synthesis (50-300mg daily)
  4. Berberine Moderate Evidence — Blood sugar regulation (500-1500mg daily (divided doses))
  5. Garlic Moderate Evidence — Cardiovascular health (600mg-2g daily)
  6. Ginkgo Biloba Moderate Evidence — Peripheral circulation (120-240mg daily (standardized extract EGb 761))
  7. Kanna Moderate Evidence — Mood enhancement (25-100mg daily (standardized extract))
  8. Red Yeast Rice Moderate Evidence — Reduced vascular cognitive impairment risk (1200-2400mg daily (standardized extract))
Strong Evidence

Tier 1 — Strong Evidence

Ashwagandha

Withania somnifera

300-600mg daily (standardized extract; 600mg/day optimal for stress/anxiety per dose-response MA) Strong Evidence
HPA axis modulation Cortisol reduction (SMD = -1.18, 23 RCTs) GABA signaling enhancement Neuroprotective effects

stress

Very large (SMD = -5.88, 22 RCTs dose-response MA; SMD = -0.95 in diagnosed disorders)

anxiety

Very large (SMD = -6.87, 22 RCTs dose-response MA; SMD = -1.13 in diagnosed disorders)

depression

Very large (SMD = -5.68, 22 RCTs dose-response MA; SMD = -1.28 in diagnosed disorders)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Drowsiness, GI upset, Headache

B-vitamin complex

RDA levels to therapeutic doses (varies by B vitamin) Strong Evidence
Neurotransmitter synthesis support Homocysteine metabolism Myelin sheath maintenance DNA synthesis and repair

stress

Moderate (SMD=0.35, Long & Benton 2013)

memory

Small to moderate (particularly in deficient)

homocysteine

Large reduction

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Rare: nausea, Urine discoloration (B2)

1,3,7-Trimethylxanthine

50-400mg daily Strong Evidence
Adenosine receptor antagonism Dopamine enhancement Adrenaline stimulation Calcium release in muscles

alertness

Large improvement

reaction_time

exercise_performance

Moderate improvement

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Jitters, Anxiety, Insomnia

Citicoline

CDP-Choline

250-1000mg daily Strong Evidence
Phosphatidylcholine synthesis enhancement Acetylcholine production increase Dopamine receptor density improvement Membrane phospholipid synthesis

memoryImprovement

Large (d = 0.6-0.9)

attentionEnhancement

Moderate to large (d = 0.5-0.8)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Mild headache, Insomnia (if taken late), GI upset (rare)

Creatine

N-methylguanidino acetic acid

3-5g daily (maintenance) or 20g daily for 5 days (loading) Strong Evidence
ATP regeneration Brain energy metabolism Neuroprotection Cellular energy buffering

memory

Small to moderate (83.3% of studies positive in aging; particularly strong in vegetarians)

strength

Moderate to large (significant via 69-study meta-analysis; RVE-confirmed)

bodyComposition

Moderate (FFM +1.39 kg, body mass +0.89 kg; dose-response from 61 trials)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Water retention, Weight gain, GI upset if loading

Folate

Pteroylglutamic acid/5-MTHF

400–800 mcg/day standard; 800 mcg–5 mg/day for special populations (MTHFR carriers, therapeutic depression, cerebral folate deficiency) Strong Evidence
One-carbon metabolism via MTHFR/DHFR enzymatic pathway Homocysteine remethylation to methionine via methionine synthase DNA synthesis and repair via thymidylate synthesis Folate receptor alpha (FRα) mediated brain transport

ntd_prevention

~70% risk reduction (population data)

homocysteine

Large reduction (combined B-vitamin trials)

cognitive_aging

Moderate (Lancet 2007 RCT: 3.5-year cognitive benefit in older adults)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Rare: mild GI upset at high doses

Iron

Iron (Fe)

8-65mg daily; RDA 8-18mg; therapeutic 25-65mg; athletes 100mg/day; bisglycinate preferred Strong Evidence
Oxygen transport via hemoglobin Cellular energy production (electron transport chain) Neurotransmitter synthesis (dopamine, serotonin) DNA synthesis support

cognitive_function

Moderate to large in iron deficiency (d=0.46 intelligence, d=0.53 short-term memory; Fiani 2025)

energy_levels

Large improvement in deficiency (fatigue d=0.34 RCT, d=1.01 pre-post; Fiani 2025)

exercise_performance

2-20% endurance improvement, 6-15% VO2max improvement (Pengelly 2024, 23 studies)

Safety Profile

● Fair. Common: Constipation, Stomach upset, Nausea

Magnesium

Magnesium (various forms)

200-400mg daily (elemental magnesium) Strong Evidence
GABA receptor modulation NMDA receptor antagonism Muscle relaxation Neurotransmitter regulation

sleep

Moderate (significant improvement in elderly)

stress

Small to moderate

bloodPressure

Small-moderate (SBP -2.81 mmHg, DBP -2.05 mmHg overall; up to -7.68 mmHg in hypertensives on medication; 38 RCTs)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Diarrhea, GI upset, Nausea

Melatonin

N-acetyl-5-methoxytryptamine

0.5-3mg, 30-60 minutes before bedtime Strong Evidence
Melatonin receptor activation Circadian rhythm regulation Antioxidant effects Sleep-wake cycle modulation

sleepOnsetLatency

SOL SMD=-0.37 (Choi 2022 MA, 11 RCTs); dose-response peaks at 4mg/day (Cruz-Sanabria 2024)

sleepQuality

PSQI WMD=-1.24 (Gholami 2022 MA, 23 RCTs, p<0.001)

icuDelirium

RR=0.72 (Tang 2025 MA, 32 RCTs, n=3895, 95% CI: 0.58-0.89)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Daytime drowsiness, Headache, Dizziness

depression

2.4μg RDA to 1000μg therapeutic Strong Evidence
Myelin sheath maintenance Homocysteine metabolism DNA methylation Neurotransmitter synthesis

memory

mood

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Rare: injection site reactions

Vitamin C

Ascorbic acid

500mg-2g daily; optimal antioxidant and immune benefit at 500-1000mg Strong Evidence
Powerful antioxidant activity (MDA reduction, TAC increase, GPx enhancement) Collagen synthesis cofactor Immune system modulation Neurotransmitter synthesis support

antioxidant_status

MDA significantly reduced, TAC and GPx significantly improved (Moabedi 2025, 17 RCTs, n=965)

immune_function

Cold duration reduced 8% adults, 14% children (Hemilä 2013); COVID-19 mortality OR=0.64 (Qin 2024, 22 studies)

cancer_prevention

Colorectal cancer RR=0.55, breast cancer RR=0.72, prostate cancer RR=0.88 (Arshadi 2024, 69 studies)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: GI upset (high doses), Kidney stones (very high doses)

Vitamin D3

Cholecalciferol

1000-4000 IU daily (based on blood levels; 2000-4000 IU optimal for metabolic benefit) Strong Evidence
Vitamin D receptor activation Calcium homeostasis Gene expression regulation Immune modulation (innate and adaptive)

mood

Small to moderate (in deficient individuals)

bone

Moderate (fracture prevention)

immune

Moderate in deficient populations (OR 0.58 at <25 nmol/L; Jolliffe 2025, 46 RCTs, n=64,573)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Hypercalcemia (high doses), Kidney stones (rare)

Zinc

Zinc (elemental)

8-40mg daily; therapeutic range 15-30mg; bisglycinate preferred for absorption Strong Evidence
Enzyme cofactor (300+ enzymes and 2000+ proteins) Protein structure stabilization (zinc finger motifs) Gene expression regulation Neurotransmitter metabolism (dopamine, serotonin, GABA)

immune_function

Moderate improvement (deficient individuals)

cognitive_function

Small to moderate (in deficiency)

wound_healing

Moderate improvement

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Nausea (empty stomach), Metallic taste, Copper deficiency (high doses)

Moderate Evidence

Tier 2 — Moderate Evidence

5-HTP

5-hydroxytryptophan

50-300mg daily Moderate Evidence
Serotonin synthesis precursor — bypasses rate-limiting tryptophan hydroxylase step Crosses blood-brain barrier without active transport competition Converts to serotonin via aromatic L-amino acid decarboxylase Sleep-wake cycle regulation via melatonin precursor pathway

mood

Small to moderate

sleep

Moderate improvement

Safety Profile

● Moderate — serotonin syndrome risk with concurrent serotonergic medications requires supervision. Common: Nausea, Diarrhea, Drowsiness

Acetyl-L-Carnitine

N-acetyl-L-carnitine

500-2000mg daily Moderate Evidence
Mitochondrial energy production Acetylcholine synthesis support Neurotrophin factor enhancement Oxidative stress reduction

cognition

Small to moderate — Cochrane review: benefits in some dementia/MCI studies (Hudson 2003)

depression

Moderate (adjunct) — improved antidepressant response in elderly (Zanardi 2006)

erectileFunction

PLC+ALC+Sildenafil SUCRA 97% for IIEF-EF improvement (Barbonetti 2024 NMA, 15 RCTs)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Nausea, Restlessness, Fishy body odor

Akkermansia muciniphila

Akkermansia muciniphila

1×10^9 – 1×10^10 CFU/day (pasteurized or live) Moderate Evidence
Amuc_1100 outer membrane protein activation of TLR2 signaling to strengthen gut barrier tight junctions Mucin layer restoration and intestinal barrier reinforcement reducing metabolic endotoxemia AMPK pathway activation in liver and adipose tissue improving insulin signaling Endocannabinoid system modulation reducing gut permeability and systemic inflammation

insulinSensitivity

Significant improvement in insulin sensitivity index (+0.89 vs placebo, p<0.05, pasteurized form, Depommier 2019)

insulinResistance

28.6% reduction in HOMA-IR (pasteurized Akkermansia vs placebo, 3-month RCT)

bodyWeight

Trend toward reduced body weight (-2.27 kg pasteurized vs placebo, not primary endpoint)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Mild bloating or flatulence during initial use (transient), Mild GI discomfort at high doses (rare)

Bovine Colostrum

Colostrum bovinum

0.5-20 g/day Moderate Evidence
Immunoglobulin (IgG, IgA) transfer providing passive immunity Growth factor delivery (IGF-1, TGF-beta, EGF) supporting gut epithelial repair Lactoferrin-mediated antimicrobial activity Tight junction protein support reducing intestinal permeability

upperRespiratorySymptomDays

44% reduction (rate ratio 0.56, 95% CI 0.43-0.72) -- Jones 2016 meta-analysis

upperRespiratoryEpisodes

38% reduction (rate ratio 0.62, 95% CI 0.40-0.99) -- Jones 2016 meta-analysis

exerciseInducedGutPermeability

80% truncation of rise -- Marchbank 2011 RCT

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Mild GI discomfort, Bloating, Nausea (rare)

Centella Asiatica

Centella asiatica

300-900mg daily (standardized extract) Moderate Evidence
Improved cerebral circulation Antioxidant neuroprotection Collagen synthesis enhancement GABA receptor modulation

memoryImprovement

Moderate (cognitive test scores 15-25% improvement)

circulationEnhancement

Moderate microcirculation improvement

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Drowsiness, Nausea, Skin irritation (topical)

Choline

Choline bitartrate/CDP-Choline

250mg-2g daily Moderate Evidence
Acetylcholine synthesis precursor Phospholipid membrane synthesis Methylation pathway support Neurotransmitter production

cognitive_function

Moderate improvement (deficient individuals)

memory

Small to moderate effect

liver_function

Moderate improvement

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Fishy body odor (high doses), GI upset

Chondroitin Sulfate

Chondroitin sulfate sodium

800-1200mg daily (pharmaceutical-grade CS only) Moderate Evidence
Cartilage matrix MMP/ADAMTS suppression Anti-inflammatory NF-κB pathway inhibition Synovial fluid enhancement Proteoglycan and aggrecan synthesis support

joint_pain

Small to moderate (SMD 0.18-0.35; pharmaceutical-grade only)

structural_jsn

Significant JSN reduction (6 RCTs, Hochberg 2008 meta-analysis)

mobility

Small improvement

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: GI upset, Nausea, Heartburn

Chromium

Chromium picolinate

200-400mcg daily Moderate Evidence
Enhanced insulin receptor binding Glucose transporter activation Improved glucose uptake Insulin sensitivity enhancement

glucoseControl

Small to moderate (HbA1c reduction 0.5-1%)

insulinSensitivity

Moderate in insulin-resistant populations

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Rare: skin irritation, GI upset (high doses)

Cinnamon Extract

Cinnamomum cassia

120mg-6g daily (extract or powder) Moderate Evidence
Enhanced insulin sensitivity Glucose transporter activation Alpha-glucosidase inhibition Anti-inflammatory prostaglandin modulation

glucoseReduction

Small to moderate (10-29mg/dL fasting glucose)

HbA1c

Small reduction (0.09-0.83%)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Mouth irritation, Allergic reactions (rare), Liver concerns (high coumarin doses)

Collagen Peptides

Hydrolyzed Collagen (Types I, II, III)

2.5-15 g/day (most common: 5-10 g/day) Moderate Evidence
Stimulation of fibroblast proliferation and collagen synthesis in dermis Upregulation of procollagen type I and elastin biosynthesis Osteoblast stimulation and bone formation marker (P1NP) increase Chondrocyte stimulation and cartilage matrix synthesis

skinWrinkles

20% reduction in eye wrinkle volume vs placebo (n=114, 8 weeks)

procollagenISynthesis

65% increase vs placebo (Proksch 2014b)

elastinSynthesis

18% increase vs placebo (Proksch 2014b)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Mild GI discomfort (rare), Mild nausea (rare), Unpleasant taste or aftertaste

CoQ10

Coenzyme Q10

100-300mg daily Moderate Evidence
Mitochondrial energy production Antioxidant protection Cell membrane stabilization Endothelial function improvement

heart_function

Moderate improvement — mortality RR=0.64 (36% reduction), hospitalization RR=0.50 (Xu 2024 MA, 33 RCTs)

energy

Small to moderate (in deficient individuals)

exerciseMuscleProtection

Dose-dependent reduction in CK (WMD=-50.64 IU/L), LDH (WMD=-52.10 IU/L) per 100mg/day (Talebi 2024 MA, 28 RCTs, n=830, GRADE moderate-high)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Rare: GI upset, Insomnia (if taken late)

ATP and oxygen utilization enhancement Mitochondrial function improvement Adaptogenic stress response Adenosine receptor modulation

exercise_performance

Moderate improvement

energy_levels

oxygen_utilization

Zingiber officinale

nausea_reduction

inflammation_markers

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Heartburn, Stomach upset, Diarrhea

Ginkgo Biloba

Ginkgo biloba

120-240mg daily (standardized extract EGb 761) Moderate Evidence
Cerebral blood flow enhancement Platelet aggregation inhibition Antioxidant neuroprotection Neurotransmitter modulation

dementiaCognition

Small to moderate improvement at 6 months (Wieland 2026 Cochrane, 82 RCTs, low certainty)

mciCognition

Probably no effect (Wieland 2026 Cochrane, moderate certainty)

vciCognition

Large effect d=0.83 (95% CI 0.00-1.67) for GB extracts, ranked #1 (Masserini 2025, 173 trials)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Headache, Dizziness, GI upset

Glucosamine

Glucosamine sulfate

1500mg glucosamine sulfate daily (sulfate form preferred over HCl) Moderate Evidence
Cartilage matrix GAG and proteoglycan synthesis (in vitro evidence) NF-κB pathway inhibition — anti-inflammatory (in vitro; not confirmed in human RCTs at clinical significance) Joint space narrowing reduction (clinical evidence: modest but present for GS)

joint_pain

Sub-threshold: VAS WMD -5.7 to -7.4mm (MCID=15mm; does not meet clinical significance in most independent meta-analyses)

joint_space_narrowing

Small but significant: SMD -0.42 (Gregori 2018, industry-conflicted); GS alone significant in Rabade 2024

function

WOMAC not significantly improved in independent meta-analyses

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Mild GI upset, Nausea, Heartburn

Glutathione

L-Glutathione (reduced)

250-1000 mg/day oral; 500-1000 mg/day liposomal Moderate Evidence
Direct scavenging of reactive oxygen species (ROS) and reactive nitrogen species (RNS) Glutathione peroxidase (GPx) cofactor for hydrogen peroxide and lipid peroxide detoxification Phase II detoxification via glutathione S-transferase (GST) conjugation reactions Maintenance of thiol redox balance (GSH/GSSG ratio) in cells

erythrocyteGsh1000mg

30-35% increase in erythrocyte, plasma, and lymphocyte GSH at 6 months (p<0.05, RCT n=54)

buccalCellGsh1000mg

260% increase in buccal cell GSH at 6 months (p<0.05, RCT n=54)

nkCellCytotoxicity1000mg

>2-fold increase in NK cell cytotoxicity at 3 months vs placebo (RCT n=54)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: Mild flatulence, Loose stools, Occasional flushing

GlyNAC

Glycine + N-Acetyl-L-Cysteine

2.4-14 g/day total GlyNAC (1:1 glycine:NAC ratio) Moderate Evidence
Glutathione synthesis restoration via rate-limiting precursors glycine and cysteine Mitochondrial fatty-acid oxidation improvement Reactive oxygen species neutralization through enhanced GSH availability NF-kB-mediated inflammation reduction

mitochondrialFattyAcidOxidation

30% improvement (p<0.001, T2D pilot, n=10)

insulinResistance

22% decrease (p<0.01, T2D pilot, n=10)

plasmaFreefattyAcids

25% reduction (p<0.01, T2D pilot, n=10)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Mild GI discomfort at higher doses

fastingGlucoseT2DM

fatOxidation

500-1000mg daily (standardized extract) Moderate Evidence

cognition_mmse

alzheimers_prevention

Significant MMSE and CASI improvement vs placebo (Li 2020 RCT, n=41, 49 weeks)

acute_cognition

Improved Stroop task performance within 60 min of single dose (Docherty 2023 RCT, n=41)

Maca Root

Lepidium meyenii

1,500-3,500 mg/day (gelatinized or dried root powder) Moderate Evidence
Adaptogenic regulation of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis Non-hormonal modulation of sexual function independent of testosterone and estradiol Macamide and macaene bioactive compound activity Antioxidant and free radical scavenging activity

sexualDesire

Significant improvement at 8-12 weeks vs placebo, independent of hormonal changes (Gonzales 2002, n=57)

erectileFunction

Significant IIEF-5 improvement vs placebo (Zenico 2009, n=50, p<0.05)

ssriSexualDysfunction

Remission rates exceeding placebo for desire and orgasm endpoints (Dording 2015, n=45)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Mild gastrointestinal symptoms (occasional), Generally well tolerated in all clinical trials up to 12 weeks

Magnesium L-Threonate

Magnesium L-threonate

1,000-2,000 mg/day magnesium L-threonate Moderate Evidence
Elevation of brain magnesium concentration via BBB-permeable threonate transport Increased hippocampal synaptic density and synaptogenesis Enhancement of NMDA receptor signaling and synaptic plasticity Upregulation of NR2B-containing NMDA receptors in prefrontal cortex

overallCognitionImpairedAdults

overallCognitionHealthyAdults

Significant improvement (p = 0.043)

reactionTime

Significant improvement (p = 0.031)

fmriAttentionResponse

postoperativeDeliriumPrevention

Nicotinamide riboside/Nicotinamide mononucleotide

250-500mg NR or NMN daily Moderate Evidence
NAD+ biosynthesis via Preiss-Handler pathway (gut microbiota conversion) Sirtuin activation (SIRT1-7) Mitochondrial biogenesis and function PARP1/CD38 competition for NAD+

nad_blood_levels

muscle_grip_strength

systolic_blood_pressure

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Mild nausea, Mild flushing at high doses, Transient headache

NMN (Nicotinamide Mononucleotide)

β-Nicotinamide mononucleotide

250-900 mg/day; most evidence at 250 mg/day for 8-12 weeks Moderate Evidence
NAD+ biosynthesis precursor (direct substrate for NMNAT enzymes) Sirtuin activation via NAD+ replenishment (SIRT1, SIRT3, SIRT6) Mitochondrial function enhancement PARP-mediated DNA repair support

nadElevation

Approximately 2-fold increase at 1000 mg twice daily (Pencina 2023); significant at all doses 250+ mg

insulinSensitivity

Significant improvement in muscle insulin-stimulated glucose disposal (Yoshino 2021, p<0.05 vs placebo)

aerobicCapacity

Dose-dependent improvement in ventilatory threshold (Liao 2021)

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Minimal; occasional mild GI discomfort

Palmitoylethanolamide

Palmitoylethanolamide (PEA)

300-1200 mg/day Moderate Evidence
PPAR-alpha receptor activation NF-kB signaling inhibition Mast cell stabilization and degranulation inhibition Microglial and astrocyte activation reduction

chronicPainPooled

SMD 1.68 (95% CI 1.05-2.31, p=0.00001) from meta-analysis of DB-RCTs (Lang-Illievich 2023)

painClinicalResponse

81% of PEA patients achieved pain score <=3 vs 40.9% controls by day 60 (Paladini 2016)

bdnfIncrease

d=0.62 medium-large effect (Briskey 2024, 700 mg/day for 6 weeks)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: No significant adverse effects reported across controlled trials

Eurycoma longifolia

3,8-Dihydroxy-6H-dibenzo[b,d]pyran-6-one

muscleStrength

muscleEndurance

inflammation

Significant reduction in CRP levels vs placebo

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Mild GI discomfort, Headache (rare)

Pyridoxine/Pyridoxal-5-phosphate

1.3-1.7mg RDA to 50-100mg therapeutic Moderate Evidence
Neurotransmitter synthesis (serotonin, dopamine, GABA) Homocysteine metabolism Amino acid metabolism Gene expression regulation

mood

Moderate (PMS, depression)

neurotransmitters

Large increase

Safety Profile

● Good. Common: Neuropathy (high doses >100mg), Photosensitivity

Vitamin E

α-tocopherol (primary active form); also tocotrienols

15mg/day RDA; 200 IU/day for immune benefit; 2000 IU/day for AD under specialist supervision; CAUTION: ≥400 IU/day increases all-cause mortality Moderate Evidence
Lipid peroxidation inhibition (chain-breaking antioxidant in cell membranes and LDL) T-cell and antibody response enhancement (immunomodulation) Antioxidant neuroprotection — mitochondrial oxidative stress reduction in neurons

immuneFunction

Large (65% DTH increase at 200 IU/day; Meydani 1997)

alzheimersFunctional

Moderate (3.15-unit ADCS-ADL improvement; Dysken 2014)

cardiovascularMortality

Null to harmful (RR 1.13 heart failure; HR 1.74 hemorrhagic stroke at ≥400 IU/day)

Safety Profile

● Caution. Common: GI upset at high doses, Increased bleeding time

Whey Protein

Bovine milk serum protein (alpha-lactalbumin, beta-lactoglobulin, immunoglobulins)

20-40g post-exercise; 40g/day for muscle maintenance in elderly Moderate Evidence
mTOR/p70S6K1 pathway activation via leucine Muscle protein synthesis stimulation (fast-digesting leucine-rich profile) Glutathione precursor provision (cysteine, glutamate) IGF-1 pathway modulation

muscle_protein_synthesis

Large (post-exercise vs. carbohydrate)

lean_mass_elderly_RET

Moderate (SMD ~0.3-0.5 vs. placebo+RET)

body_composition_healthy_adults

Small-Moderate (context-dependent)

Safety Profile

● Excellent. Common: GI discomfort at high doses, Bloating in lactose-sensitive individuals

Zeaxanthin

Zeaxanthin

2-10mg daily Moderate Evidence
Macular pigment density increase Blue light filtration Antioxidant protection Anti-inflammatory effects

macularDensity

Large (significant macular pigment increase)

visualFunction

Moderate (contrast sensitivity and glare reduction)

● Excellent. Common: Yellowing of skin (high doses), No significant side effects

750-1500mg daily (where legal) Preliminary Evidence

anxietyLongTerm

Maintained lower GAD symptoms vs placebo (P=0.0032, n=93; Mao 2016)

depressionReduction

Significant HAM-D reduction vs placebo (P<0.05, n=57; Amsterdam 2012)

300-450 mg/day (standardized extract with echinacoside) Preliminary Evidence

DIM (3,3'-Diindolylmethane)

3,3'-Diindolylmethane

100-300mg daily (absorption-enhanced formulation); typical clinical dose 150mg BID Preliminary Evidence
Modulates estrogen metabolism by shifting 2-hydroxylation:16a-hydroxylation ratio via CYP1A1/CYP1A2 induction Aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AhR) agonist/modulator affecting xenobiotic metabolism NF-kB signaling inhibition reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine expression (TNF-a, IL-6, IL-1b) Nrf2/ARE pathway activation upregulating detoxification enzymes (NQO1, GST, HO-1)

estrogenMetaboliteRatioShift

2-OHE1:16a-OHE1 ratio increase of +3.2 vs -0.7 placebo (P<0.001, n=98; Thomson 2017)

shbgIncrease

Significant SHBG increase vs placebo in tamoxifen-treated women (P<0.05, n=98; Thomson 2017)

cervicalDysplasiaCINRegression

Complete CIN regression significantly higher vs placebo at 100mg and 200mg vaginal DIM (P<0.05, n=multisite; Ashrafian 2015)

Mechanisms of Action

Understanding the pharmacological mechanisms through which supplements interact with drugs and body systems is essential for predicting and avoiding adverse interactions.

CYP450 Enzyme Inhibition & Induction

The cytochrome P450 enzyme family metabolizes approximately 75% of all prescription drugs. Supplements that inhibit (e.g., berberine inhibits CYP2D6 and CYP3A4) or induce (e.g., St. John's Wort induces CYP3A4) these enzymes can dramatically alter drug blood levels, causing toxicity or therapeutic failure.

Key supplements: Berberine, Quercetin, Resveratrol, Ginkgo Biloba

Serotonergic Potentiation

Supplements that increase serotonin levels (5-HTP, SAMe, kanna) or inhibit serotonin reuptake create additive risks when combined with serotonergic medications (SSRIs, SNRIs, triptans, MAOIs). Serotonin syndrome is a potentially fatal condition requiring emergency medical attention.

Key supplements: 5-HTP, SAMe, Kanna, Rhodiola rosea

Anticoagulant & Antiplatelet Effects

Multiple supplements have blood-thinning properties through various mechanisms: platelet aggregation inhibition (ginkgo, omega-3), vitamin K antagonism (high-dose vitamin E), or fibrinolytic activity (nattokinase). Combining these with prescription anticoagulants (warfarin, apixaban) or antiplatelet agents (aspirin, clopidogrel) increases bleeding risk.

Key supplements: Ginkgo Biloba, Omega-3 Fatty Acids, Garlic, Vitamin E

Thyroid & Endocrine Modulation

Several supplements affect thyroid hormone levels, cortisol production, or sex hormone metabolism. Ashwagandha increases thyroid hormones, while supplements like DIM and calcium D-glucarate alter estrogen metabolism. These effects can interfere with hormone-sensitive conditions and medications.

Key supplements: Ashwagandha, Berberine, Red Yeast Rice, Vitamin D3

Dosage Guidelines

The following dosage ranges reflect those used in published clinical trials. Individual needs may vary. Always start with the lower end and titrate upward as tolerated.

Supplement Clinical Dosage Range Evidence Tier Safety Rating
Ashwagandha 300-600mg daily (standardized extract; 600mg/day optimal for stress/anxiety per dose-response MA) Strong Evidence Good
Omega-3 Fatty Acids 1000-2000mg daily (combined EPA/DHA) Strong Evidence Excellent
5-HTP 50-300mg daily Moderate Evidence Moderate — serotonin syndrome risk with concurrent serotonergic medications requires supervision
Berberine 500-1500mg daily (divided doses) Moderate Evidence Good
Garlic 600mg-2g daily Moderate Evidence Good
Ginkgo Biloba 120-240mg daily (standardized extract EGb 761) Moderate Evidence Good
Kanna 25-100mg daily (standardized extract) Moderate Evidence Good
Red Yeast Rice 1200-2400mg daily (standardized extract) Moderate Evidence Moderate

Note: Dosage ranges are derived from clinical trial protocols and may not account for individual variation. Consult a healthcare provider for personalized dosing.

Safety & Interactions

The following safety notes represent the most critical, highest-risk interactions and contraindications across the supplement database. This is not exhaustive — consult individual supplement monographs for complete safety profiles.

  • CRITICAL — Serotonin syndrome: Never combine 5-HTP, SAMe, or kanna with SSRIs, SNRIs, MAOIs, triptans, or tramadol without medical supervision. Symptoms include confusion, rapid heart rate, high blood pressure, dilated pupils, muscle twitching, and hyperthermia. Seek emergency care immediately if suspected.
  • CRITICAL — Bleeding risk: Ginkgo biloba, omega-3 fatty acids (>3g/day), garlic, and vitamin E have anticoagulant effects. Discontinue all at least 2 weeks before surgery. Do not combine with warfarin, heparin, or novel oral anticoagulants without physician monitoring.
  • CRITICAL — Statin interactions: Red yeast rice contains monacolin K (identical to lovastatin) and should NEVER be combined with prescription statins due to additive risk of rhabdomyolysis. Berberine also interacts with statin metabolism via CYP3A4 inhibition.
  • Pregnancy contraindications: Many supplements lack adequate pregnancy safety data. Known contraindications include: ashwagandha (possible abortifacient), berberine (uterine contractions), high-dose vitamin A (teratogenic), kanna (no safety data). Always consult OB/GYN before any supplement use during pregnancy.
  • Immunosuppressant interactions: Immune-stimulating supplements (reishi, astragalus, echinacea, vitamin D at high doses) may counteract immunosuppressant medications used in organ transplant recipients and autoimmune conditions. This interaction can have life-threatening consequences.
  • Pre-surgical disclosure: All supplements must be disclosed to anesthesiologists before surgery. Common problematic supplements include valerian (potentiates anesthetics), ginkgo (bleeding), garlic (bleeding), and St. John's Wort (reduces effectiveness of many anesthetic agents).

Individual Supplement Safety Profiles

Supplement Common Side Effects Key Drug Interactions Safety Rating
Ashwagandha Drowsiness, GI upset, Headache Sedatives, Immunosuppressants, Thyroid medications Good
Omega-3 Fatty Acids Fishy aftertaste, GI upset, Burping Blood thinners, Blood pressure medications Excellent
5-HTP Nausea, Diarrhea, Drowsiness SSRIs, SNRIs, MAO inhibitors Moderate — serotonin syndrome risk with concurrent serotonergic medications requires supervision
Berberine GI upset, Diarrhea, Constipation Diabetes medications, Blood thinners, Antibiotics Good
Garlic Garlic odor, GI upset, Heartburn Blood thinners, HIV medications Good
Ginkgo Biloba Headache, Dizziness, GI upset Blood thinners, Seizure medications, Diabetes drugs Good
Kanna Mild sedation, Headache, Nausea (high doses) SSRIs, MAO inhibitors, Cardiac glycosides Good
Red Yeast Rice Muscle pain, Digestive upset, Headache Statins, Anticoagulants, Cyclosporine Moderate

Research Gaps & Limitations

While the evidence base for safety & interactions supplements continues to grow, several important gaps remain:

  • Systematic drug-supplement interaction databases are incomplete — many potential interactions have never been formally studied
  • Most supplement safety data comes from short-term trials (8-12 weeks) — long-term chronic use safety is largely unknown
  • Pharmacokinetic interaction studies between popular supplement combinations are essentially non-existent
  • Population-specific safety (elderly, pediatric, renally impaired) is poorly characterized for most supplements
  • Contamination and adulteration of supplement products remains a significant safety concern not captured in efficacy trial data
  • Reporting bias means adverse events from supplements are significantly underreported compared to pharmaceuticals

Citation Index

All citations verified against PubMed. PMID links resolve to PubMed; DOI links resolve to the publisher. Grouped by supplement.
